meeting: Post Quantum Transaction Signature #11 - Formal Verification

formal_verification_progress.highlight: Ricardo (EPFL student) formally verified Riva Labs' FORS+ verifier contract in Lean4 via EVMYulLean (Nethermind tool), proving correct address recovery for all possible inputs
Proof runs on optimized Yul IR, not EVM bytecode — Solidity compiler backend (Yul→bytecode) remains trusted but unverified
AI used to write Lean4 proofs and reference model; correctness relies on Lean kernel, not AI output. Riva Labs' reference Lean model also AI-assisted from Solidity + FORS+ context
Verity team reportedly achieved full formal refinement of SPHINCS+; separate effort to formally verify SLH-DSA wallet integration is in progress

verification_scope_and_limitations.highlight: Contract proves which address signed — validity check (recovered address == expected address) must be done by the calling contract; unforgeability not claimed
keccak assumed correct (long-standing EVM primitive); not formally verified in this work
Ground truth risk: Lean reference model derived from Solidity+AI; manual review needed to confirm it matches NIST/FIPS specification intent
Yoichi: security proof anchoring recover function to hash pre-image hardness would strengthen confidence in Lean spec independent of PDF comparison
Benedikt: CryptHOL framework (Lean4) enables formalizing asymptotic and concrete security proofs for hash-based signatures; security definition itself still needs human review

action_items.action: Extend verification to cover EVM bytecode (not just optimized Yul), and add security reduction proof (breaking FORS+ implies breaking keccak pre-image)

decisions.decision: Priority: proving security assumptions (ground truth strength) ranked higher than completing Yul→bytecode verification gap
